basic_details.job_title: NPSC CTSE Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for 172 Junior Engineer, Foreman and More Posts
basic_details.short_information: The Nagaland Public Service Commission (NPSC) has released the NPSC CTSE 2026 notification (Advertisement No. NPSC-2/CTSE-2026, dated 25 June 2026) for filling up vacancies across 49 item numbers covering multiple technical posts under the Government of Nagaland. Candidates aged 21 to 32 years can register online from 27 June 2026 (12:00 noon) to 17 July 2026 (12:00 noon). The examination fee is ₹300 plus ₹25 convenience fee and applicable payment gateway charges.

application_fee.details: The application fee for the NPSC Combined Technical Services Examination 2026 is straightforward and uniform. The OTR (One Time Registration) itself is completely free of cost - candidates only pay at the examination application stage. The total payable amount is ₹300 as examination fee plus ₹25 as convenience fee, along with applicable payment gateway charges.

age_limit.details: Category Minimum Age Maximum Age General 21 years 32 years SC / ST 21 years 37 years (5-year relaxation) Serving Government Employees 21 years Up to 5 additional years based on service length

qualification.details: Every candidate applying for the NPSC CTSE 2026 must meet both the age limit and the post-specific educational qualification as on 1 January 2026. The qualifications must have been obtained from a recognised university or institution at the time of applying - degrees in progress are not accepted. Candidates claiming tribal or indigenous inhabitant reservations must hold the appropriate certificates signed by an authority not below the rank of Additional Deputy Commissioner.

post_wise_qualification.essential_qualification: MBBS from a recognised university recognised by MCI/NMC

Bachelor's Degree in Physics, Chemistry, Botany, Zoology, Geology, Forestry, Agriculture, Horticulture, Biochemistry, Biotechnology, Veterinary & Animal Husbandry, Mathematics, Statistics, or Mechanical Engineering. Preference given to B.Sc/M.Sc (Forestry); 50% posts reserved for Forestry graduates
Master's Degree in Statistics/Economics/Mathematics/Commerce, or a graduate with one of these subjects + 3 years' experience as Inspector of Statistics
Bachelor's Degree with Honours in Statistics/Economics/Mathematics/Commerce, or graduate with these subjects + 3 years' experience as SIS/Field Investigator
B.V.Sc & A.H / B.V.Sc + Registration Certificate from Indian Veterinary/Nagaland Veterinary Council
Graduate in any discipline + LLB; registered with a Bar Council; 2 years' practice at Bar
Degree in Civil Engineering recognised by AICTE
3-year Diploma in Civil Engineering recognised by AICTE
3-year Diploma in Mechanical Engineering recognised by AICTE
Degree or 3-year Diploma in Architecture recognised by AICTE
Graduate in Electrical Engineering / Electrical & Electronics Engineering
Diploma in Electrical Engineering / Electrical & Electronics Engineering
B.E/B.Tech in IT/Computer Science & Engineering / B.Sc IT / B.Sc Computer Science from AICTE/UGC recognised university
B.E/B.Tech in IT/CS/Electronics & Telecom Engineering or MCA or M.Sc Computer Science

Bachelor's/Master's/Doctorate in Dairy Technology, Biotechnology, Oil Technology, Veterinary Sciences, Biochemistry, Microbiology, Chemistry, Medical Science, Food Technology, or Agricultural Science disciplines; plus completed training as specified by the Food Authority
M.Sc with min. 50% in Forensic Science/Chemistry/Toxicology
M.Sc with min. 50% in Zoology/Botany/Biochemistry/Microbiology/Biotechnology/Physical Anthropology/Molecular Biology/Forensic Science
B.E Computer/B.E IT/MCA/M.Sc IT
MBBS from MCI/NMC recognised university
BDS

salary_details.level 13: Regimental Medical Officer, Asst. Conservator of Forests, Economics & Statistics Officer, Veterinary Assistant Surgeon, Asst. Public Prosecutor, SDO (Civil/Electrical), Medical Officer, JE (Civil) Backlog 2021, Asst. Urban Dev. Officer (Backlog), Asst. Architect
salary_details.level 12: SDO posts (some), Superintendent (Economic Plant), Vice-Principal Grade-II
salary_details.level 11: JE (Civil/Electrical/Mechanical), Asst. Programme Officer, SDO (Civil) Power, Food Safety Officer, Junior Soil Conservation Officer, Agriculture Officer, JE (Civil) PHE, Asst. Programmer, JE (Survey), JE (Mining), Junior Architect
salary_details.level 8: Inspector of Statistics, Junior Architect (Works & Housing), Foreman, Planning Assistant, Sectional Overseer, Modeler, Draftsman, Vocational Instructors, Demonstrators, Pharmacists, Fishery Inspectors, Agriculture Inspector, Caretaker, Asst. Electrical Inspector
salary_details.details: Selected candidates for NPSC CTSE 2026 posts are placed on the Government of Nagaland's Pay Matrix levels as specified in the notification. Class-I Gazetted posts (such as Regimental Medical Officer, Assistant Conservator of Forests, SDO posts, Veterinary Assistant Surgeon) are placed at Pay Matrix Level 13, which represents the highest pay tier in this recruitment. Class-II Gazetted posts are generally at Level 11 or 12, while Class-III Non-Gazetted posts are at Level 8.

selection_process: The NPSC CTSE selection process consists of two stages: a written competitive examination and an interview/personality test. Candidates are assessed first on the written exam
and only those who qualify are called for the interview stage. The exact exam pattern and syllabus for CTSE-2026 can be downloaded from the Commission's website at https://npsc.nagaland.gov.in.

Candidates sit for a written exam that includes both a general paper and an elective subject paper specific to the post they have applied for. Each item number has a designated elective subject - for example
Item 1 (Regimental Medical Officer) requires Medical Science as the elective
Item 4 (Veterinary Assistant Surgeon) requires Veterinary Science
and Item 6(a) (SDO Civil Engineering) requires Civil Engineering (Degree). The exact syllabus and examination pattern are available on the NPSC portal for download.

Candidates shortlisted on the basis of the written examination are called for an interview conducted by the Commission. All original documents must be produced at the time of the interview; candidates who fail to bring originals will not be permitted to appear.
The final merit list is prepared based on combined performance in the written examination and interview. NPSC's decision on all matters relating to eligibility
and selection is final and binding.
how_to_apply: The NPSC CTSE Apply Online process is entirely web-based, and no offline application form will be issued from the Commission's office. Candidates must first complete the OTR (One Time Registration), which is mandatory for all applicants. Only after OTR is confirmed can candidates proceed to submit the examination application form. The online application portal is available at https://npsc.nagaland.gov.in from 27 June 2026.
Visit the official NPSC portal at https://npsc.nagaland.gov.in
Click on the OTR (One Time Registration) link - available from 27 June 2026 at 12:00 noon
Complete the One Time Registration (OTR is free of charge) - the OTR window closes on 15 July 2026 at 12:00 noon
After OTR confirmation, log in and navigate to the CTSE-2026 online application form
Fill in all required personal and educational details and select the relevant item number(s) for which you are eligible
Upload all required documents - educational certificates, caste/tribe certificate, Indigenous Inhabitant Certificate, photograph, and NOC if applicable
Pay the examination fee of ₹300 + ₹25 convenience fee plus gateway charges through the online payment gateway
Submit the application form before 17 July 2026 at 12:00 noon
Download and save the confirmation/printout of the submitted application form for future reference
Government employees must upload a No Objection Certificate (NOC) signed by their Head of Department while submitting the online application.

content_sections.heading: NPSC CTSE 2026 Notification PDF Download
content_sections.text: Direct link to the NPSC official notification: Click Here The notification covers all 49 item numbers including fresh vacancies (2026) and backlog vacancies from 2016, 2017, 2018, 2021, 2023, 2024, and 2025. Candidates should check the specific reservation category against each item number before deciding which posts to apply for.

content_sections.heading: Quick Summary - NPSC Combined Technical Services Examination 2026
Important Dates for NPSC CTSE 2026
content_sections.text: Candidates planning to apply for the NPSC Combined Technical Services Examination 2026 must track these deadlines carefully, as the application and OTR windows close at 12:00 noon sharp and not at midnight. The OTR (One Time Registration) closes two days before the application deadline, so candidates should register early. Missing the OTR window means being unable to submit the main application form.

content_sections.heading: NPSC CTSE 2026 Vacancy Breakdown - Post-wise Details
content_sections.text: The NPSC CTSE Vacancy 2026 spans a wide range of technical disciplines, from medical and engineering to forestry, law, and allied sciences. Posts are distributed across fresh vacancies (Items 1–25) and backlog vacancies from years 2016 through 2025 (Items 26–49). Each post carries specific tribal and category reservations as per Government of Nagaland rules, with 80% of vacancies reserved for Indigenous Inhabitants of Nagaland and specified Backward Tribes. Fresh Vacancies (Items 1–25) 135 Posts

Candidates claiming tribal or indigenous inhabitant reservations must hold the appropriate certificates signed by an authority not below the rank of Additional Deputy Commissioner.

content_sections.heading: Application Fee - NPSC CTSE 2026
content_sections.text: The application fee for the NPSC Combined Technical Services Examination 2026 is straightforward and uniform. The OTR (One Time Registration) itself is completely free of cost - candidates only pay at the examination application stage. The total payable amount is ₹300 as examination fee plus ₹25 as convenience fee , along with applicable payment gateway charges.

content_sections.heading: How to Apply Online - NPSC CTSE 2026 Apply Online
content_sections.text: The NPSC CTSE Apply Online process is entirely web-based, and no offline application form will be issued from the Commission's office. Candidates must first complete the OTR (One Time Registration), which is mandatory for all applicants. Only after OTR is confirmed can candidates proceed to submit the examination application form. The online application portal is available at https://npsc.nagaland.gov.in from 27 June 2026. Step-by-step application process: Visit the official NPSC portal at https://npsc.nagaland.gov.in Click on the OTR (One Time Registration) link - available from 27 June 2026 at 12:00 noon Complete the One Time Registration (OTR is free of charge) - the OTR window closes on 15 July 2026 at 12:00 noon After OTR confirmation, log in and navigate to the CTSE-2026 online application form Fill in all required personal and educational details and select the relevant item number(s) for which you are eligible Upload all required documents - educational certificates, caste/tribe certificate, Indigenous Inhabitant Certificate, photograph, and NOC if applicable Pay the examination fee of ₹300 + ₹25 convenience fee plus gateway charges through the online payment gateway Submit the application form before 17 July 2026 at 12:00 noon Download and save the confirmation/printout of the submitted application form for future reference Government employees must upload a No Objection Certificate (NOC) signed by their Head of Department while submitting the online application.

content_sections.heading: Pay Scale - NPSC CTSE 2026 Salary
content_sections.text: Selected candidates for NPSC CTSE 2026 posts are placed on the Government of Nagaland's Pay Matrix levels as specified in the notification. Class-I Gazetted posts (such as Regimental Medical Officer, Assistant Conservator of Forests, SDO posts, Veterinary Assistant Surgeon) are placed at Pay Matrix Level 13 , which represents the highest pay tier in this recruitment. Class-II Gazetted posts are generally at Level 11 or 12 , while Class-III Non-Gazetted posts are at Level 8 .

content_sections.heading: Selection Process - NPSC Combined Technical Services Examination 2026
content_sections.text: The NPSC CTSE selection process consists of two stages: a written competitive examination and an interview/personality test. Candidates are assessed first on the written exam, and only those who qualify are called for the interview stage. The exact exam pattern and syllabus for CTSE-2026 can be downloaded from the Commission's website at https://npsc.nagaland.gov.in . Candidates sit for a written exam that includes both a general paper and an elective subject paper specific to the post they have applied for. Each item number has a designated elective subject - for example, Item 1 (Regimental Medical Officer) requires Medical Science as the elective, Item 4 (Veterinary Assistant Surgeon) requires Veterinary Science, and Item 6(a) (SDO Civil Engineering) requires Civil Engineering (Degree). The exact syllabus and examination pattern are available on the NPSC portal for download. Candidates shortlisted on the basis of the written examination are called for an interview conducted by the Commission. All original documents must be produced at the time of the interview; candidates who fail to bring originals will not be permitted to appear. The final merit list is prepared based on combined performance in the written examination and interview. NPSC's decision on all matters relating to eligibility, conduct of examination, and selection is final and binding.

content_sections.heading: Documents Required for NPSC CTSE 2026
content_sections.text: Candidates must have all necessary documents ready both at the time of online application and when called for interview. Missing or invalid documents at any stage - including discovered suppression of information - will lead to cancellation of candidature. The Commission has specified that all certificates submitted must have been issued by the concerned authority on or before the last date of application. The following documents are required: HSLC/Matriculation/Equivalent Admit Card (for Date of Birth proof - no other certificate accepted for age) Indigenous Inhabitant Certificate (if applicable) Scheduled Tribe Certificate (if applicable) Backward Tribe Certificate (if applicable - signed by authority not below rank of Additional Deputy Commissioner) Mark Sheets and Pass Certificates from Matriculation through the requisite qualification Registration Certificate (for Veterinary Assistant Surgeon and similar posts requiring Council registration) Certificate from competent medical authority (for PwBD candidates) No Objection Certificate from Head of Department (for serving Government employees, to be uploaded at application stage) Proof of Bar registration and practice (for Assistant Public Prosecutor)

content_sections.heading: Troubleshooting and Common Mistakes to Avoid
content_sections.text: Candidates applying for NPSC CTSE 2026 frequently make errors that result in rejection even after appearing in the examination. Being aware of these pitfalls before applying saves time, effort, and the ₹325 fee. Confusing OTR deadline with application deadline: The OTR closes on 15 July 2026, two days before the application deadline of 17 July 2026. Candidates who miss OTR cannot submit the application - complete OTR first and immediately. Applying for the wrong reservation category: Each item number has specific reservation tags (Unreserved, Indigenous Inhabitant, specific Backward Tribes). Carefully check whether your tribe or category matches the reservation for the post before applying. Using wrong age proof documents: The notification explicitly states that only the HSLC/Matriculation/Equivalent Admit Card is accepted for date of birth proof. Any other certificate - including a birth certificate - will not be accepted. Not having Council registration ready: Posts like Veterinary Assistant Surgeon and Pharmacist require a valid registration certificate from the relevant State Council. Apply only if you already hold this registration. Government employees not uploading NOC: Serving government employees who submit their application without uploading the Head of Department's NOC risk disqualification even if they pass the exam. Submitting an application after 12:00 noon on the last day: The portal closes at exactly 12:00 noon on 17 July 2026, not at midnight. Last-minute submissions after noon will not be accepted. Missing elective subject match: Each item number specifies an elective subject for the exam. Ensure the elective subject listed for your chosen item matches your qualification - for instance, Item 6(a) requires Civil Engineering (Degree), not Diploma. Incorrect or incomplete tribe certificates: Tribe and Indigenous Inhabitant certificates must be signed by an authority not below the rank of Additional Deputy Commissioner. Certificates signed by lower-ranking officials are invalid.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for NPSC Combined Technical Services Examination 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the online application form for NPSC CTSE 2026 is 17 July 2026 at 12:00 noon. The OTR (One Time Registration), which is a mandatory prerequisite, closes earlier on 15 July 2026 at 12:00 noon.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are notified in NPSC CTSE 2026?
faq.answer: The NPSC CTSE 2026 notification covers 49 item numbers containing multiple posts across fresh vacancies (2026) and backlog vacancies from 2016, 2017, 2018, 2021, 2023, 2024, and 2025 under various departments of the Government of Nagaland.

faq.question: Q3. What is the age limit for NPSC CTSE 2026?
faq.answer: The age limit for NPSC CTSE 2026 is a minimum of 21 years and a maximum of 32 years, calculated as on 01 January 2026. SC/ST candidates get a relaxation of 5 years, making their upper limit 37 years. Serving government employees also get age relaxation equal to their years of service, up to a maximum of 5 years.

faq.question: Q4. What is the application fee for NPSC CTSE 2026?
faq.answer: The examination fee is ₹300 plus a convenience fee of ₹25, along with applicable payment gateway charges. The OTR (One Time Registration) is free of cost.

faq.question: Q5. What is the selection process for NPSC CTSE 2026?
faq.answer: The selection process consists of two stages: a written competitive examination followed by an interview/personality test. Candidates who qualify the written exam are called for the interview. NPSC's decisions on all selection matters are final.

faq.question: Q6. Is OTR mandatory for NPSC CTSE 2026?
faq.answer: Yes, OTR (One Time Registration) is mandatory for all candidates appearing in CTSE-2026. OTR is free of charge and must be completed between 27 June 2026 and 15 July 2026 at 12:00 noon. Without completing OTR, candidates cannot submit the examination application form.

faq.question: Q7. What is the official website for NPSC CTSE 2026?
faq.answer: The official website is https://npsc.nagaland.gov.in. Both OTR and the online application form are available on this portal. For queries, candidates can call the NPSC helpdesk at 9402988506 during office hours (10:00 to 15:00).

faq.question: Q8. What documents are needed for NPSC CTSE 2026?
faq.answer: Required documents include the Matriculation/HSLC Admit Card for age proof, Indigenous Inhabitant/ST/BT certificates (signed by an authority not below Additional Deputy Commissioner rank), educational mark sheets and pass certificates, and an NOC from the Head of Department for government employees. PwBD candidates also need a certificate from a competent medical authority.

faq.question: Q9. What pay level do NPSC CTSE 2026 selected candidates get?
faq.answer: Selected candidates are placed on the Government of Nagaland Pay Matrix. Class-I Gazetted posts such as Regimental Medical Officer, Veterinary Assistant Surgeon, and SDO posts are at Pay Matrix Level 13. Junior Engineer posts are generally at Level 11, and Class-III non-gazetted posts such as Foreman, Pharmacist, and Sectional Overseer are at Level 8.

faq.question: Q10. Can candidates from outside Nagaland apply for NPSC CTSE 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates from outside Nagaland may apply for unreserved posts, but 80% of the vacancies are reserved for Indigenous Inhabitants of Nagaland belonging to specified groups (any Naga Tribe, Kuki, Kachari, Garo, or Mikir) who are indigenous inhabitants of the State. Backward Tribe reserved posts are strictly for candidates from the specified tribes mentioned against each item number.You said: Refer to the provided PDF and write the article in a unique, original, and SEO-friendly manner based only on verified facts from the source.

mainEntity.name: Q10. Can candidates from outside Nagaland apply for NPSC CTSE 2026?

acceptedAnswer.text: Candidates from outside Nagaland may apply for unreserved posts, but 80% of the vacancies are reserved for Indigenous Inhabitants of Nagaland belonging to specified groups (any Naga Tribe, Kuki, Kachari, Garo, or Mikir) who are indigenous inhabitants of the State. Backward Tribe reserved posts are strictly for candidates from the specified tribes mentioned against each item number.You said: Refer to the provided PDF and write the article in a unique, original, and SEO-friendly manner based only on verified facts from the source.

mainEntity.name: What is the last date to apply for NPSC Combined Technical Services Examination 2026?

acceptedAnswer.text: The last date to submit the online application form for NPSC CTSE 2026 is 17 July 2026 at 12:00 noon. The OTR (One Time Registration), which is a mandatory prerequisite, closes earlier on 15 July 2026 at 12:00 noon.

mainEntity.name: How many vacancies are notified in NPSC CTSE 2026?

acceptedAnswer.text: The NPSC CTSE 2026 notification covers 49 item numbers containing multiple posts across fresh vacancies (2026) and backlog vacancies from 2016, 2017, 2018, 2021, 2023, 2024, and 2025 under various departments of the Government of Nagaland.

mainEntity.name: What is the age limit for NPSC CTSE 2026?

acceptedAnswer.text: The age limit for NPSC CTSE 2026 is a minimum of 21 years and a maximum of 32 years, calculated as on 01 January 2026. SC/ST candidates get a relaxation of 5 years, making their upper limit 37 years. Serving government employees also get age relaxation equal to their years of service, up to a maximum of 5 years.

mainEntity.name: What is the application fee for NPSC CTSE 2026?

acceptedAnswer.text: The examination fee is ₹300 plus a convenience fee of ₹25, along with applicable payment gateway charges. The OTR (One Time Registration) is free of cost.

mainEntity.name: What is the selection process for NPSC CTSE 2026?

acceptedAnswer.text: The selection process consists of two stages: a written competitive examination followed by an interview/personality test. Candidates who qualify the written exam are called for the interview. NPSC's decisions on all selection matters are final.

mainEntity.name: Is OTR mandatory for NPSC CTSE 2026?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, OTR (One Time Registration) is mandatory for all candidates appearing in CTSE-2026. OTR is free of charge and must be completed between 27 June 2026 and 15 July 2026 at 12:00 noon. Without completing OTR, candidates cannot submit the examination application form.

mainEntity.name: What is the official website for NPSC CTSE 2026?

acceptedAnswer.text: The official website is https://npsc.nagaland.gov.in. Both OTR and the online application form are available on this portal. For queries, candidates can call the NPSC helpdesk at 9402988506 during office hours (10:00 to 15:00).

mainEntity.name: What documents are needed for NPSC CTSE 2026?

acceptedAnswer.text: Required documents include the Matriculation/HSLC Admit Card for age proof, Indigenous Inhabitant/ST/BT certificates (signed by an authority not below Additional Deputy Commissioner rank), educational mark sheets and pass certificates, and an NOC from the Head of Department for government employees. PwBD candidates also need a certificate from a competent medical authority.

mainEntity.name: What pay level do NPSC CTSE 2026 selected candidates get?

acceptedAnswer.text: Selected candidates are placed on the Government of Nagaland Pay Matrix. Class-I Gazetted posts such as Regimental Medical Officer, Veterinary Assistant Surgeon, and SDO posts are at Pay Matrix Level 13. Junior Engineer posts are generally at Level 11, and Class-III non-gazetted posts such as Foreman, Pharmacist, and Sectional Overseer are at Level 8.
